title: HDFS block verification failure (Event 29)
text: Verification of block blk_5210986793132641744 failed. Checksum mismatch on DataNode: 10.250.19.102:50010. Block corruption detected.
root_cause: Disk block degradation on target DataNode hardware causing verification failure of block checksum.
resolution: NameNode automatically triggers block replication from a redundant healthy replica to replace the corrupted block, followed by decommissioning / scanning target disk sector on the faulty DataNode.
logs:
081111 101621 28169 INFO dfs.DataNode$DataXceiver: Receiving block blk_5210986793132641744 src: /10.250.19.102:54106 dest: /10.250.19.102:50010
081111 101622 28169 INFO dfs.DataNode$BlockReceiver: Receiving block blk_5210986793132641744 src: /10.250.19.102:54106 dest: /10.250.19.102:50010
081111 101623 28172 ERROR dfs.DataNode$BlockReceiver: Verification of block blk_5210986793132641744 failed
081111 101624 28172 WARN dfs.DataNode$DataXceiver: Block blk_5210986793132641744 is corrupt. Reporting corruption to NameNode.

title: DataNode Write Socket Timeout Exception (Event 22)
text: WriteException: Connection reset by peer on DataNode 10.250.11.23. Exception during block stream write to pipeline replica.
root_cause: Network packet drop and TCP socket timeout on target replication pipeline member.
resolution: Re-construct the write pipeline by removing the unresponsive DataNode from the pipeline list, reporting it to NameNode, and writing directly to the remaining healthy DataNodes.
logs:
081109 203518 143 INFO dfs.DataNode$DataXceiver: Receiving block blk_-1608999687919862906 src: /10.250.11.23:54106 dest: /10.250.11.24:50010
081109 203525 143 WARN dfs.DataNode$BlockReceiver: Exception in receiveBlock for block blk_-1608999687919862906 java.io.IOException: Connection reset by peer
081109 203526 143 INFO dfs.DataNode$DataXceiver: Abandoning replication pipeline member /10.250.11.24:50010 due to write failure.

title: PacketResponder Exception Storm (Event 5)
text: PacketResponder Exception for block blk_3892019485732103. Terminating responder thread. DataNode cannot send ACK packet to client.
root_cause: Buffer pool allocation exhaustion or heap pressure on the target DataNode JVM runtime.
resolution: Restart the target DataNode JVM process, adjust heap allocation configuration (-Xmx4g), and trigger temporary load shedding / routing.
logs:
081112 041209 1082 INFO dfs.DataNode$PacketResponder: PacketResponder 1 for block blk_3892019485732103 terminating
081112 041210 1082 ERROR dfs.DataNode$PacketResponder: Exception PacketResponder 1 for block blk_3892019485732103 java.io.EOFException
081112 041212 1083 WARN dfs.DataNode$DataXceiver: PacketResponder exception on client stream. Aborting connection handler.

title: NameNode Block Report Latency Spike (Event 16)
text: NameNode processing block report from DataNode 10.250.5.12 took 14200ms (threshold 1000ms). RPC queues overloaded.
root_cause: GC pause storm or database metadata lock saturation on NameNode due to high metadata update rate.
resolution: Enable NameNode fair-call queue scheduler, trigger metadata checkpointing to save FSImage, and scale auxiliary standby NameNode to distribute active query load.
logs:
081110 112001 12 INFO dfs.FSNamesystem: Rollback log processing complete. Active NameNode running.
081110 112015 15 WARN org.apache.hadoop.ipc.Server: IPC Server Responder: slow response processing blockReport from 10.250.5.12:50010 took 14.2s
081110 112016 15 INFO dfs.FSNamesystem: BLOCK* NameSystem.processReport: Block report parsing slow down client queue count: 284

title: DataNode Decommissioning & Block Replication Storm
text: DataNode 10.250.32.8 decommissioned. Under-replicated blocks count: 18450. Re-replication queue processing at maximum bandwidth.
root_cause: Sudden removal of node with large active blocks count triggering massive network transmission spikes across healthy peers.
resolution: Throttle the replication stream using 'dfs.namenode.replication.max-streams' configurations, monitor packet-loss metrics, and balance the cluster nodes.
logs:
081114 021530 45 INFO dfs.FSNamesystem: Decommissioning DataNode 10.250.32.8 started
081114 021532 45 WARN dfs.FSNamesystem: Block blk_2391058295321045 has only 1 replica. Under-replicated blocks queue size: 18450
081114 021535 48 INFO dfs.FSNamesystem: Triggering parallel copy tasks for block re-replication.

title: DataNode Block Allocation Target Mismatch
text: Failed to place replica for block blk_982103857321049. Not enough DataNodes available: 2 (required: 3). Replication policy violated.
root_cause: Network split / partition isolating rack-2 from the primary master node.
resolution: Establish target route checks, check cluster switch status, and dynamically drop replication constraints temporarily if strict rack-safety is not required.
logs:
081115 142055 120 INFO dfs.FSNamesystem: BLOCK* NameSystem.allocateBlock: /user/root/testfile blk_982103857321049
081115 142056 120 WARN org.apache.hadoop.hdfs.server.namenode.BlockPlacementPolicy: Failed to place replica. Target nodes: 2, required: 3.
081115 142058 120 ERROR dfs.FSNamesystem: Out of space or missing healthy DataNodes for block blk_982103857321049 allocation.

title: DataNode Heartbeat Timeout & Death
text: DataNode 10.250.14.88:50010 has been quiet for 630 seconds. Marking DataNode as DEAD. Activating replica replication tasks.
root_cause: Kernel freeze or network interface controller (NIC) failure on the physical rack hardware hosting 10.250.14.88.
resolution: NameNode scheduler removes dead node from active list, lists blocks on dead node as under-replicated, and requests healthy nodes to trigger replication copies.
logs:
081116 091012 34 INFO dfs.FSNamesystem: Number of transactions: 12095
081116 091018 34 WARN dfs.FSNamesystem: Lost heartbeat from DataNode 10.250.14.88. Timeout 630s exceeded.
081116 091020 35 INFO dfs.FSNamesystem: Removing DataNode 10.250.14.88 from active registry. Active DataNode nodes remaining: 15

title: DFS Namesystem Journal Log Sync Lag
text: Slow journal log sync to Standby NameNode. Edit Log write took 8400ms. High system write I/O operations per second (IOPS).
root_cause: Local SSD write cache exhaustion or shared storage contention on the active NameNode master host.
resolution: Optimize write buffer cache, clean up historical checkpoint logs, and migrate active metadata logging directory to dedicated local flash media storage.
logs:
081117 183301 99 INFO dfs.FSImage: Checkpointing started on Standby NameNode
081117 183312 101 WARN dfs.FSNamesystem: Slow journal log sync! Edit Log write processing slow queue latency: 8.4s
081117 183315 101 INFO dfs.FSImage: Saving FSImage checkpoints to partition disk took 12 seconds.

title: DataNode Write Pipeline Recovery Timeout
text: Pipeline recovery failed for block blk_849201857210349. Recovery attempts: 5/5. Primary DataNode replication handshakes failing.
root_cause: Clock drift or SSH / TCP port credential mismatch between the cluster nodes.
resolution: Run NTP synchronization across the cluster to align node timestamps and verify that target internal connection ports are open in security tables.
logs:
081118 221004 88 INFO dfs.DataNode$BlockReceiver: Recovering pipeline for block blk_849201857210349
081118 221010 88 WARN dfs.DataNode$BlockReceiver: Pipeline recovery attempt 5 failed. Node timestamp deviation detected.
081118 221012 89 ERROR dfs.DataNode$DataXceiver: Aborting block replication handshakes. Pipeline broken.

title: HDFS Client DFSClient Write Exception
text: DFSClient: Exception in createBlockOutputStream: org.apache.hadoop.ipc.RemoteException: All replicas are decommissioned / dead.
root_cause: Total cluster depletion where write requests cannot allocate any healthy replication targets.
resolution: Audit active cluster node states, verify that target DataNodes are registered, and scale capacity online.
logs:
081119 110544 12 INFO dfs.DFSClient: Creating new output stream for block blk_492018572103495
081119 110545 12 WARN dfs.DFSClient: RemoteException: All replica nodes are quiet, decommissioned, or dead.
081119 110547 13 ERROR dfs.DFSClient: Failed to allocate target write block stream. Terminating client connection.
